title: Educating Clients on Platform Access
content:
Overview


Clients access the platform through different login paths depending on their account type. When a client is confused about how to log in, agents should first identify whether they are a B2C Premium user or a School user, then walk them through the correct path.



B2C Premium Login Path


Individual subscribers follow these steps:


  1. Go to tklesson.com
  2. Click Premium Self-Study
  3. Click Already Subscribed? Log In
  4. Enter email address and password
  5. If 2FA is enabled, enter the 6-digit code from their authenticator app
  6. After successful authentication, they land on the Premium Dashboard

Common confusion points:


  • Can't find the login page: The login is accessed via the Premium page, not the main homepage. Walk them to tklesson.com → Premium Self-Study → Already Subscribed? Log In.
  • Forgot password: The login screen has a "Forgot password?" link that sends a reset email.
  • 2FA issues: If they can't access their authenticator app, there is a "Use a recovery code instead" option. If recovery codes are also lost, refer to the Disabling 2FA article.


School Portal Login Path


School admins, teachers, students, and parents all begin at the same School Login page. After login, they land on the School Hub where they select their portal based on their role.


Step 1 — Login:


  1. Go to tklesson.com → School Login
  2. Enter email address — if the email domain matches a registered school, the school is auto-detected and shown with a green confirmation badge
  3. If not auto-detected, type the school name in the search field and select from the dropdown
  4. Enter password
  5. If 2FA is enabled, a modal appears requesting a 6-digit code or a backup code

Step 2 — School Hub:


After successful login, all users land on the School Hub which displays portal cards based on their role:


  • Students see only the Student Portal card — access to courses, study materials, quizzes, flashcards, and progress tracking
  • Teachers see only the Teacher Portal card — class management, student progress, assignments, and gradebooks
  • Parents see only the Parent Portal card — child's grades, announcements, calendar, and school communication
  • School Admins see all four cards — Student, Teacher, Admin, and Parent portals — plus a subscription status banner

If the school's subscription is inactive or the trial has ended, non-admin users will see locked cards and a message directing them to contact their school administrator.


Common confusion points:


  • "I don't know my school ID": There is no manual school ID entry. The school is either auto-detected by email domain or searched by name. No memorization needed.
  • "My school doesn't appear in the search": The school may not be registered, or the name typed doesn't match the official school name. Agents can verify via User Lookup.
  • "I'm at the wrong school": If the user selects a school they're not registered at, they'll receive an error. They must select the correct school.
  • "I only see one portal card — where are the others?": Non-admin users only see the card for their role. Only school admins see all four portals. This is by design.
  • "My portal card is locked": The school's subscription may be inactive or the trial may have ended. Only school admins bypass this restriction. Non-admin users should contact their school administrator.
  • "I have both a personal premium account and a school account": These are separate. Use Premium Self-Study → Log In for personal access, and School Login for school access.
  • Parents not yet registered: If a parent hasn't been added to the school system, they should use the "Applying to a School for Your Child?" link at the bottom of the School Login page to submit an application.


Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Let me walk you through exactly where to click. Tell me what you see on your screen right now."
  • "Your school should appear when you start typing the name — try the first few letters."
  • "After you log in, you'll see a hub with your portal. Click it to enter."
  • "If your card is locked, that means the school's subscription needs attention — only your school admin can fix that."
  • "Now that you know the path, you'll be able to do this on your own next time."
